commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Windows Node 24.0-24.14 compile-cache skip to `openclaw.mjs` and `src/entry.compile-cache.ts`, with focused unit and launcher e2e coverage.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +184, Other +15. Total +230 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. the linked issue has concrete Windows/npm-global steps, but this read-only review did not run native Windows or reproduce the hang. Source and release inspection show the affected compile-cache path is still enabled on current main and v2026.5.28.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is native Windows package proof and maintainer acceptance of the scoped compatibility tradeoff, not a mechanical code repair.

target.title: fix(cli): skip compile cache on early Node 24.x to avoid startup deadlock
target.body:
## Summary

Disable Node compile cache for Windows packaged startup on early Node 24.x versions (24.0-24.14), where the linked issue was reported on a native Windows npm-global install.

This applies the same skip to both the TypeScript packaged entry decision and the `openclaw.mjs` packaged launcher path, while keeping compile cache enabled for non-Windows packaged installs on Node 24.0-24.14 and for Windows packaged installs on Node 24.15+ and other Node majors.

Fixes #86550

## Root Cause

- Root cause: OpenClaw enables Node's compile cache during packaged CLI startup before importing the full CLI. The linked issue reports a startup hang on native Windows npm-global with early Node 24.x at that entry stage. The source of the failure is the startup-level `enableCompileCache(...)` decision before command dispatch, not gateway, plugin, channel, provider, or command-specific code.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch blocks Node's compile-cache API for Windows packaged startup on Node 24.0-24.14 in both `src/entry.compile-cache.ts` and the `openclaw.mjs` packaged launcher path. That removes the reported entry-level deadlock trigger for the affected Windows packaged startup class while preserving the existing compile-cache contract for non-Windows packaged installs and Windows Node 24.15+.
- Fix classification: root-cause startup gate fix for Windows packaged startup on early Node 24, with an explicit proof gap for native Windows npm-global after-fix behavior.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: Medium-high for the source boundary and regression tests; medium overall because maintainer-side or authorized native Windows npm-global proof is still required before merge.
- Why it matters / User impact: If the reported hang reproduces, affected Windows users cannot get basic CLI commands like `openclaw --version`, `openclaw doctor --fix`, or `openclaw gateway start` to exit. The update protects Windows packaged startup on early Node 24 without taking compile-cache performance away from macOS/Linux users on the same Node minors.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: native Windows npm-global CLI startup can hang on Node 24.1.0 when Node compile cache is enabled before the CLI import path; the code fix applies to Windows packaged startup on Node 24.0-24.14, including the `openclaw.mjs` packaged launcher.
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 local checkout with focused compile-cache decision tests and launcher-level packaged startup tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:core:test`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: The entry compile-cache Vitest shard passed 18 tests. The launcher e2e Vitest shard passed 27 tests with 2 platform skips. The tests prove Windows Node 24.1.0 and 24.14.0 packaged startup skips compile cache; Windows Node 24.15.0 still enables it; and Linux/macOS early Node 24 packaged startup still enables it.
- Observed result after fix: The compile-cache skip is scoped to Windows packaged startup on early Node 24 in both the entry compile-cache gate and `openclaw.mjs`, instead of disabling compile cache for every platform on Node 24.0-24.14.
- What was not tested: No native Windows npm-global after-fix run was available in this environment, so `openclaw --version`, `openclaw doctor --fix`, and `openclaw gateway start` were not executed on Windows + Node 24.1.0. Local Linux proof is not a substitute because the requested proof is native Windows packaged CLI startup before gateway-specific behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`; `test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: early Node 24.x disables compile cache on Windows packaged installs and on the packaged launcher, stays enabled for early Node 24.x on Linux/macOS packaged startup, and stays enabled for Node 24.15+ / Node 22 / Node 25 on Windows packaged startup.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the changed behavior is a startup decision before command execution; injecting `nodeVersion` and `platform` directly proves the Windows packaged startup decision boundary without requiring the test runner itself to run on each OS and Node minor.

## Scope and architecture

- What did NOT change: No unrelated gateway behavior, plugin/channel/provider code, config surface, dependency, lockfile, docs, or unrelated startup path changed. The only behavior change is the compile-cache startup default for Windows packaged Node 24.0-24.14; unrelated CLI startup hangs remain out of scope.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The source-of-truth boundary is split by runtime artifact: `src/entry.compile-cache.ts` is the canonical typed entry helper, and `openclaw.mjs` is the canonical packaged launcher pre-import compile-cache path. Both now apply the same Windows Node 24.0-24.14 contract before calling `enableCompileCache(...)` or respawning for packaged compile cache.
- Compatibility / related open PR scan: the compatibility-sensitive surface is packaged CLI startup defaulting, and this patch reduces the current PR's blast radius. `check-upstream-fixed` reported current `origin/main` does not already cover this PR, with no overlapping main changes in `src/entry.compile-cache.ts` or `src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-002 / RF-003 / RF-006: Still require native Windows npm-global proof for Node 24.0-24.14. This update does not claim that proof; it keeps the proof gap explicit.
- RF-004: Fixed by narrowing the runtime guard to Windows packaged startup, so macOS/Linux packaged installs on Node 24.0-24.14 keep compile cache enabled.
- RF-005: Maintainer decision is still needed for final Windows proof acceptance, but the code no longer makes the broader all-platform tradeoff.
- RF-007: Covered by focused tests proving Windows Node 24.15+ still allows compile cache in both entry and launcher startup paths.
- RF-008: Covered by `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts` and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts`.

## Patch quality notes

- Patch quality notes: The added `return false` is the intentional safe branch for Windows packaged startup on early Node 24: do not call Node's compile-cache API when it can hang before any CLI command logic runs. The default for every other packaged runtime remains the existing compile-cache-enabled path. No new dependencies, config knobs, fallback stacks, or runtime shims were added.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: compatibility`.
- Risk explanation: Availability risk is the reported Windows startup hang. Compatibility/performance risk is changing the packaged startup compile-cache default. The updated patch limits that default change to Windows Node 24.0-24.14 only.
- Why acceptable: Compile cache is a startup optimization, while the reported failure prevents the CLI from starting at all. Preserving compile cache outside the Windows early-Node-24 boundary keeps the fix narrow and makes the remaining tradeoff explicit for maintainers to validate with native Windows proof.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
